title: Setup
text: CohortContrast package designed facilitate cohort exploration. accepts target cohort size OMOP CDM instance allows provide custom control cohort generate one using matching inverse controls. package performs concept-level enrichment analysis provides handy GUI visualization concept mapping.

title: Setup
code: library(CohortContrast) library(CDMConnector) library(DBI) library(RPostgres) library(dplyr) library(tibble)

title: Setup
text: CDMConnector package used establish database connection running CohortContrast. can configure connection either reading credentials .Renviron file explicitly writing script.
code: ################################################################################ # # Initiate the database connection # #################################################################################  port <- as.integer(Sys.getenv("DB_PORT"))  cdmSchema <-   Sys.getenv("OHDSI_CDM") # Schema which contains the OHDSI Common Data Model cdmVocabSchema <-   Sys.getenv("OHDSI_VOCAB") # Schema which contains the OHDSI Common Data Model vocabulary tables. cdmResultsSchema <-   Sys.getenv("OHDSI_RESULTS") # Schema which contains "cohort" table (is not mandatory) writeSchema <-   Sys.getenv("OHDSI_WRITE") # Schema for temporary tables, will be deleted writePrefix <- "cc_"  db = DBI::dbConnect(   RPostgres::Postgres(),   dbname = Sys.getenv("DB_NAME"),   host = Sys.getenv("DB_HOST"),   user = Sys.getenv("DB_USERNAME"),   password = Sys.getenv("DB_PASSWORD"),   port  = port )  cdm <- CDMConnector::cdmFromCon(   con = db,   cdmSchema = cdmSchema,   achillesSchema = cdmResultsSchema,   writeSchema = c(schema = writeSchema, prefix = writePrefix) )

what: Configuring Python dependencies (for GUI and summary precompute)
title: Setup
text: plan use interactive viewer (runCohortContrastViewer()) summary precompute (precomputeSummary()), configure Python install required Python packages:

title: Setup
text: deploying offline air-gapped environment, see supplementary article Air-gapped server setup: vignette("a10_air_gapped_server_setup", package = "CohortContrast")

title: Setup
text: Let´s say want explore cohort lung cancer patients. can import target cohort multiple ways:

what: 1. Target cohort from OHDSI OMOP database.
title: Setup
text: cohort defined ATLAS, can use unique cohort ID. Ensure cohort generated within ATLAS CDM instance.
code: ################################################################################ # # Create target table from OMOP CDM instance (ATLAS's cohort id) # #################################################################################  cohortsTableName = 'cohort' targetCohortId = 1403  targetTable <- CohortContrast::cohortFromCohortTable(cdm = cdm, db = db,    tableName = cohortsTableName, schemaName = cdmResultsSchema, cohortId = targetCohortId)

what: 2. Target cohort from JSON description file.
title: Setup
text: JSON expression cohort (exportable ATLAS), can import cohort directly. package also ships example JSON cohort inst/example/example_json/cohort.json (installed path shown ).
code: ################################################################################ # # Create target table from a JSON # ################################################################################# pathToJSON = '/path/to/atlas_json' targetTable <- CohortContrast::cohortFromJSON(pathToJSON = pathToJSON, cdm = cdm) # Example bundled with CohortContrast: exampleJsonDir <- system.file("example/example_json", package = "CohortContrast") list.files(exampleJsonDir, pattern = "\\\\.json$", full.names = TRUE) # [1] ".../example_json/cohort.json"  targetTable <- CohortContrast::cohortFromJSON(pathToJSON = exampleJsonDir, cdm = cdm)

what: 3. Target cohort from a CSV file.
title: Setup
text: can import target table CSV. file columns present inside cohort table (cohort_definition_id, subject_id, cohort_start_date, cohort_end_date).

title: Setup
text: can also use table memory. cases 2-4 specify cohortId parameter inside function call, multiple cohorts present advised.

title: Setup
text: control cohort cohort target cohort compared . means check proportions concept occurrence two cohorts. result analysis heavily dependent , therefore selected care. control cohort can generated way target cohort shown generated examples 1-4. automatic ways package CohortContrast provides:

title: Setup
text: One scientific approaches select matches database (based age sex). parameters ratio shown many controls want case. Also min (least many matches error otherwise) max (maximum n matches) parameters can used.
code: ################################################################################ # # Create control cohort table based on matches # ################################################################################# controlTable = CohortContrast::createControlCohortMatching(cdm = cdm, targetTable = targetTable, ratio = 4)

what: 2. Control cohort based on inverse controls
title: Setup
text: Sometimes makes sense use inverse controls (subjects observation period described target cohort). case example want see contrast diagnosis.
code: ################################################################################ # # Create control cohort table based on inverse controls # ################################################################################# controlTable = CohortContrast::createControlCohortInverse(cdm = cdm, targetTable = targetTable)

title: Setup
text: constructed cohorts hand strongly advised check resolve overlap conflicts well conflicts observation period inside OMOP CDM.
code: ################################################################################ # # Resolve conflicts # ################################################################################# targetTable = CohortContrast::resolveCohortTableOverlaps(cdm = cdm, cohortTable = targetTable) controlTable = CohortContrast::resolveCohortTableOverlaps(cdm = cdm, cohortTable = controlTable) # Close DB connection when done DBI::dbDisconnect(db)

title: Execution
text: Now initiated database connection created targetTable well controlTable ready execute study.
code: ################################################################################ # # Execute # ################################################################################  data = CohortContrast::CohortContrast(   cdm,   targetTable = targetTable,   controlTable = controlTable,   pathToResults = file.path(getwd(), "studies"),   domainsIncluded = c(     "Drug",     "Condition",     "Measurement",     "Observation",     "Procedure",     "Visit",     "Visit detail",     "Death"   ),   prevalenceCutOff = 2.5,   topK = FALSE, # Number of features to export   presenceFilter = 0.2, # 0-1, percentage of people who must have the chosen feature present   complementaryMappingTable = NULL, # Optional manual concept mapping table   getSourceData = FALSE, # If true will generate summaries with source data as well   runChi2YTests = TRUE,   runLogitTests = FALSE,   createOutputFiles = TRUE,   complName = "LungCancer_1Y")

title: Execution
text: multiple parameters can tweak different outcomes:

title: Execution
text: cdm Connection database targetTable Table target cohort controlTable Table control cohort pathToResults Path results folder, can project’s working directory domainsIncluded list CDM domains include, choose Drug, Condition, Measurement, Observation, Procedure, Visit, Visit detail, Death complName Name output study directory

title: Execution
text: runChi2YTests boolean running CHI2Y tests (chi-squared tests two proportions Yates continuity correction) runLogitTests boolean logit-tests prevalence, builds model predicting whether patient target control getAllAbstractions boolean creating abstractions’ levels imported data, useful using GUI exploring data maximumAbstractionLevel Maximum level abstraction allowed, getAllAbstractions TRUE, hierarchy concept_hierarchy table used getSourceData boolean fetching source data, data abstraction level used map OMOP CDM prevalenceCutOff numeric FALSE, set, removes concepts present (target) prevalenceCutOff times. Eg set 2, concepts present double target exported. topK numeric FALSE, set, keeps maximum number features analysis. Maximum number features exported. presenceFilter numeric FALSE, set, removes features represented fewer target cohort subjects given percentage complementaryMappingTable data frame NULL. Mapping table concept merges. Columns: CONCEPT_ID, CONCEPT_NAME, NEW_CONCEPT_ID, NEW_CONCEPT_NAME, ABSTRACTION_LEVEL, TYPE numCores Number cores allocate parallel processing, default max number cores - 1 createOutputFiles Boolean creating output files, default value TRUE runRemoveTemporalBias boolean optional temporal-bias reduction step main workflow runAutomaticHierarchyCombineConcepts boolean optional hierarchy-based post-processing runAutomaticCorrelationCombineConcepts boolean optional correlation-based post-processing

title: Execution
text: using GUI prevalenceCutOff, presenceFilter can changed slider. effect runChi2YTests runLogitTests can toggled filter. function output study directory complName, case LungCancer_1Y, inside pathToResults. study directory contains parquet files (example data_patients.parquet) metadata file metadata.json.

title: Execution
Graphical User Interface
text: running study createOutputFiles = TRUE (generating summaries precomputeSummary()), launch viewer:
code: exampleDataDir <- system.file("example", "st", package = "CohortContrast", mustWork = TRUE)  CohortContrast::runCohortContrastViewer(   dataDir = exampleDataDir )

title: Graphical User Interface
text: Use Studies panel pick study. badge next title confirms whether data loaded Patient Level Data mode Summary Mode.

title: Graphical User Interface
text: Recommended order: Select study confirm data mode. Set filters sidebar click Apply Filters. Review composite chart table Dashboard. Optionally refine visibility using table Show column click Apply Table Selection. Inspect Trajectories, Overlap, Demographics. Use Mappings (patient mode) concept merges review mapping history.

title: Graphical User Interface
text: Dashboard: central composite view enrichment/prevalence, time--event, age, sex, cluster prevalence columns. Trajectories: concept ordering shifts across clusters. Overlap: concept co-occurrence matrix pairwise relationship table. Demographics: cohort, cluster, concept-level age/sex summaries. Mappings: manual suggestion-based concept merges (patient mode), plus mapping history. Help: -app methods control reference. See also: Dashboard Composite Plot Trajectories Tab Overlap Tab Mappings Tab Patient vs Summary Mode Demographics Tab Sidepanel Filters Controls Air-gapped Server Setup

title: Graphical User Interface
text: patient mode, Mappings tab includes: Manual Merge: select main concepts Map column, create merged concept, apply. Hierarchy Suggestions: tune hierarchy parameters, click Update Hierarchy Suggestions, merge selected rows. Correlation Suggestions: tune correlation parameters, click Update Correlation Suggestions, merge selected rows. Mapping History: audited list applied mappings.

title: Dashboard Composite Plot
text: Dashboard primary analysis surface CohortContrast. combines effect size, prevalence, timing, demographics active concepts one plot one table.

title: Dashboard Composite Plot
text: chart concept-centric (one row per concept) combines: Event occurrences: distribution first occurrence timing. Prevalence/Enrichment: target prevalence prevalence difference ratio. Age: concept-level age estimate relative cohort context. Male proportion: concept-level sex distribution. Cluster columns: per-cluster prevalence summaries active clustering.

title: Dashboard Composite Plot
text: Apply sidebar filters inspect concepts remain active. Switch cluster view (, Cluster 1, Cluster 2, …) compare within-cluster behavior. Use table filters Show column manual inclusion/exclusion. Click Apply Table Selection persist manual visibility updates. 5. Enable ordinal rows concept’s average >2 occurrences happen.

title: Dashboard Composite Plot
text: High prevalence difference ratio solid target prevalence often indicates robust cohort contrast. Cluster-specific prevalence columns help distinguish global signals cluster-local signals. Age/sex columns can expose clinically meaningful subgroup effects potential confounding patterns.

title: Trajectories Tab
text: Trajectories tab compares concept ordering patterns across clusters using median occurrence timing prevalence.

title: Trajectories Tab
text: Overall order: ranked overall timing. Top movers: emphasizes concepts whose ordering shifts across clusters. stable: emphasizes concepts minimal ordering drift. Cluster Prevalence (%) (sidebar): specific cluster selected, filters concepts minimum within-cluster prevalence.

title: Trajectories Tab
text: Concepts move strongly clusters can indicate cluster-defining care pathways. Concepts stable ordering useful anchors comparing trajectories across cohorts/studies. Use tab together Dashboard: keep clinically relevant concepts active, inspect temporal ordering stability.

title: Overlap Tab
text: Overlap tab shows often active concepts co-occur patients.

title: Overlap Tab
text: Group selector: inspect Overall behavior specific cluster. Overlap visualization: pairwise concept relationships among currently active concepts. Pairwise table: sortable/filterable table metrics correlation, Jaccard, co-occurrence counts.

title: Overlap Tab
text: High overlap suggests concepts often appear together patients. Compare Overall versus cluster-specific overlap identify cluster-specific treatment diagnosis bundles. Use overlap findings guide concept merge decisions Mappings tab.

title: Mappings Tab
text: Mappings tab used combine concepts audit mapping decisions. Merge actions available patient mode.

title: Mappings Tab
text: Workflow: Manual Merge, select concepts using Map column (main concepts ). Click Merge Selected Concepts. Confirm New Concept ID, New Concept Name, Heritage. Click Apply Merge. Defaults merge form prefilled prevalent selected concept.

title: Mappings Tab
text: Use hierarchy-based candidate merges: Set Minimum hierarchy depth. Optionally keep Allow minors enabled. Click Update Hierarchy Suggestions. Review rows click Merge Selected Hierarchy Suggestions.

title: Mappings Tab
text: Use correlation-based candidate merges: Set Minimum correlation. Set Max median days . Optionally allow heritage drift. Click Update Correlation Suggestions. Review rows click Merge Selected Correlation Suggestions.

title: Mappings Tab
text: history table records applied mappings, including source concept IDs/names, mapped concept IDs/names, mapping type, heritage. Use audit trail post-processing decisions.

title: Patient vs Summary Mode
text: CohortContrast Viewer supports two data modes: Patient: uses patient-level parquet files (example data_patients.parquet). Summary: uses precomputed aggregate parquet files (example concept_summaries.parquet). Studies table, Mode column shows one word: Patient Summary.

title: Patient vs Summary Mode
text: Patient mode: produced directly CohortContrast(..., createOutputFiles = TRUE). Summary mode: produced precomputeSummary(studyPath = ..., outputPath = ...).
code: summary_result <- CohortContrast::precomputeSummary(   studyPath = file.path(getwd(), "studies", "LungCancer_1Y"),   outputPath = file.path(getwd(), "studies", "LungCancer_1Y_summary"),   clusterKValues = c(2, 3, 4, 5) )  # Open viewer and load the summary study CohortContrast::runCohortContrastViewer(   dataDir = file.path(getwd(), "studies") )

what: What is different in the UI
title: Patient vs Summary Mode
text: Patient: available (Manual Merge, Hierarchy Suggestions, Correlation Suggestions). Summary: disabled (history still visible). Patient: Recluster runs live clustering. Summary: clustering uses precomputed artefacts selected k filters applied. Patient: row-level patient events available. Summary: aggregate summaries available.

title: Patient vs Summary Mode
text: Use Patient mode interactive concept curation merge decisions. Use Summary mode faster sharing reproducible visualization precomputed results.

title: Demographics Tab
text: Demographics tab summarizes cohort composition concept-level demographic shifts.

title: Demographics Tab
text: KPI cards: cohort-level snapshot (patient count, age, sex proportions). Age distribution chart: cohort age profile autoscaled axis range. Cluster Demographics table: per-cluster patient count age/sex metrics. age-delta ranking table, sex-skew ranking table, filters minimum patients data source. Ordinal Progression: concept-specific age sex patterns across ordinal events.

title: Demographics Tab
text: Minimum patients: excludes low-support concepts rankings. Data source: switches overall cluster-specific demographic summaries. Ordinal concept selector: chooses concept ordinal progression charts.

title: Demographics Tab
text: Patient mode: metrics computed patient-level event data. Summary mode: metrics read precomputed summary artifacts. modes use tab layout output structure keep interpretation consistent.

title: Demographics Tab
text: Large age deltas highlight concepts concentrated younger/older subgroups. Male/female skew can reveal cohort heterogeneity intervention diagnosis context. Ordinal progression helps distinguish early vs late events longitudinal patterns.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: vignette documents left sidepanel controls Viewer interact.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: sidepanel inputs staged click Apply Filters. Moving sliders, changing heritage checkboxes, changing cluster count, editing Top N immediately recalculate active concepts. Apply Filters commit action filter state. Apply Table Selection commit action manual Show checkbox edits made Dashboard table.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Recomputes concept visibility sidepanel criteria: Heritage Types Target Prevalence range Prevalence Difference Ratio range Ordinal-row visibility rule plot-level filtering applied (cluster prevalence / Top N SD) rendering dashboard.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Persists manual table Show values active visibility state. Special rule: ordinal row set Show = TRUE, linked main concept also forced TRUE.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Runs live reclustering Patient mode. Summary mode button disabled. Summary clustering changes applied Apply Filters pressed selected cluster count.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Domain-family include/exclude control. heritage unchecked, concepts excluded. unchecked, app falls back treating “heritages selected”.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Range filter target cohort prevalence. Keeps concepts TARGET_SUBJECT_PREVALENCE_PCT inside selected range.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Range filter contrast effect size. Keeps concepts PREVALENCE_DIFFERENCE_RATIO_DISPLAY inside selected range.

what: Show ordinal data rows for active main concepts
title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Toggles whether ordinal rows shown. : ordinal rows can shown, main concepts active. : ordinal rows hidden.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Minimum prevalence threshold within currently selected cluster view. Active Dashboard view specific cluster (C1, C2, …). Inactive view .

what: Top N Concepts by SD (across clusters)
title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Keeps top N main concepts highest prevalence SD across clusters. 0 disables filter. Applied last filtering order. Ordinal rows removed filter active.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Optional cluster subset used Top N SD ranking. Empty = clusters. Select least two clusters scoped divergence ranking.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Selects cluster count (Auto, 2, 3, 4, 5). Changing input alone run clustering. Requires Recluster (patient mode) Apply Filters (summary mode).

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Controls whether clustering uses: concepts Active concepts relevant patient mode; visually disabled summary mode.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Apply Filters overrides manual table visibility edits recomputing _show filter criteria. Apply Table Selection can used filtering apply additional manual curation. Cluster count change alone nothing clustering commit action occurs. Top N SD applied filters can shrink visible concept set. Cluster Prevalence (%) applies specific selected cluster view.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Recluster enabled. Cluster assignments summaries recomputed live. Clustering scope (concepts vs Active concepts ) active.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Recluster disabled. Precomputed clustering artifacts used. Selected cluster count applied Apply Filters.

title: Sidepanel Filters and Controls
text: Set sidepanel criteria. Click Apply Filters. Optionally edit Dashboard Show values manually. Click Apply Table Selection. needed, change clustering settings commit appropriately mode.

title: Air-gapped Server Setup
text: guide covers CohortContrast deployment air-gapped servers internet access available runtime. applies Python-backed workflows: runCohortContrastViewer() (GUI) precomputeSummary() (summary-mode artifacts)

title: Air-gapped Server Setup
text: use installPythonDeps() online package indexes. must provide local wheel files (.whl) required Python dependencies. target operating system target CPU architecture target Python major/minor version validate Python environment running GUI precompute.

what: 1. Prepare wheels on a connected machine
title: Air-gapped Server Setup
text: Use machine matches target server runtime (OS, architecture, Python version). Supported offline layouts installPythonDepsOffline(): packagesDir/linux_x86_64.zip packagesDir/linux_x86_64/*.whl
code: mkdir -p /tmp/ccv_wheels/linux_x86_64 python3 -m pip download \   --dest /tmp/ccv_wheels/linux_x86_64 \   dash dash-bootstrap-components dash-ag-grid \   pandas pyarrow numpy scipy \   plotly diskcache psutil multiprocess \   scikit-learn scikit-learn-extra  # Optional: compress for transfer cd /tmp/ccv_wheels zip -r linux_x86_64.zip linux_x86_64

what: 2. Transfer wheels to the air-gapped server
title: Air-gapped Server Setup
text: Copy either: linux_x86_64.zip directory /opt/cohortcontrast/packages, wheel directory /opt/cohortcontrast/packages/linux_x86_64/*.whl.

title: Air-gapped Server Setup
text: virtual environments available: venv/ensurepip unavailable server:
code: configurePython(   pythonPath = "/usr/bin/python3",   virtualenvName = "r-cohortcontrast-viewer",   createVenv = TRUE ) configurePython(   pythonPath = "/usr/bin/python3",   createVenv = FALSE )

title: Air-gapped Server Setup
Air-gapped Server Setup
text: Confirm required modules report installed.

title: Air-gapped Server Setup
code: summaryResult <- precomputeSummary(   studyPath = "/data/studies/LungCancer_1Y",   outputPath = "/data/studies/LungCancer_1Y_summary",   clusterKValues = c(2, 3, 4, 5),   minibatchKMeansCutoffPatients = 50000 )

title: Air-gapped Server Setup
text: servers, prefer mode = "server" openBrowser = FALSE.
code: runCohortContrastViewer(   dataDir = "/data/studies",   host = "0.0.0.0",   port = 8050,   mode = "server",   logFile = "/data/studies/contrast_viewer.log",   openBrowser = FALSE,   background = TRUE )

title: Air-gapped Server Setup
text: simple: minimal logging/noise; good local ad hoc use. server: file logging stable hosted operation; recommended air-gapped servers. debug: maximum diagnostics; use troubleshooting . Optional export lockdown (common controlled environments):
code: runCohortContrastViewer(   dataDir = "/data/studies",   mode = "server",   allowExports = FALSE,   openBrowser = FALSE )

title: Air-gapped Server Setup
text: regenerate wheels server’s exact Python version, OS, architecture. use createVenv = FALSE system Python. rerun checkPythonDeps(), verify wheel directory contents, reinstall installPythonDepsOffline(). set explicit pythonPath configurePython(...). use mode = "server" set logFile explicitly. lower minibatchKMeansCutoffPatients force scalable clustering earlier.

title: Air-gapped Server Setup
text: Prepare matching wheelhouse connected machine. Transfer wheelhouse server. configurePython(...) explicit interpreter. installPythonDepsOffline(...). checkPythonDeps(). Run precomputeSummary(...) runCohortContrastViewer(...) smoke tests.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Markus Haug. Author, maintainer. Raivo Kolde. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Haug M, Kolde R (2026). CohortContrast: Feature Selection Enrichment Analysis OMOP CDM Cohorts. R package version 1.0.0, https://healthinformaticsut.github.io/CohortContrast/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {CohortContrast: Feature Selection and Enrichment Analysis for OMOP CDM Cohorts},   author = {Markus Haug and Raivo Kolde},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://healthinformaticsut.github.io/CohortContrast/}, }

what: Feature Selection and Enrichment Analysis for OMOP CDM Cohorts
title: Feature Selection and Enrichment Analysis for OMOP CDM Cohorts
text: goal CohortContrast facilitate comparison cohorts specified domains across OMOP CDM datasets. enables users analyze visualize contrast target control cohorts effectively.

title: Feature Selection and Enrichment Analysis for OMOP CDM Cohorts
text: development version package GitHub:

title: Feature Selection and Enrichment Analysis for OMOP CDM Cohorts
text: complete setup workflow code, use: Project wiki (setup + workflow): https://github.com/HealthInformaticsUT/CohortContrast/wiki Package site (function reference + vignettes): https://healthinformaticsut.github.io/CohortContrast/ running CohortContrast air-gapped server, see wiki article : https://github.com/HealthInformaticsUT/CohortContrast/wiki

title: Feature Selection and Enrichment Analysis for OMOP CDM Cohorts
text: CohortContrast package generates following outputs: Running CohortContrast returns list tables (patient level summarised data target control) saves study folder parquet files can analysed GUI directly. Using viewer helpers runCohortContrastViewer generates plots parquet-formatted results. Example studies available inst/example/st. Published studies can explored http://omop-apps.cloud.ut.ee/CohortContrast/.

title: Feature Selection and Enrichment Analysis for OMOP CDM Cohorts
text: CohortContrast provides much insight generation possibilities. See package site details: https://healthinformaticsut.github.io/CohortContrast/ feature requests create issues Github contact Markus Haug (markus.haug@ut.ee) personally.

title: Run CohortContrast Analysis — CohortContrast
text: Run CohortContrast Analysis

title: Run CohortContrast Analysis — CohortContrast
code: CohortContrast(   cdm,   targetTable = NULL,   controlTable = NULL,   pathToResults = getwd(),   domainsIncluded = c("Drug", "Condition", "Measurement", "Observation", "Procedure",     "Visit", "Visit detail", "Death"),   prevalenceCutOff = 10,   topK = FALSE,   presenceFilter = 0.005,   complementaryMappingTable = NULL,   runChi2YTests = TRUE,   runLogitTests = TRUE,   getAllAbstractions = FALSE,   getSourceData = FALSE,   maximumAbstractionLevel = 5,   createOutputFiles = TRUE,   complName = NULL,   runRemoveTemporalBias = FALSE,   removeTemporalBiasArgs = list(),   runAutomaticHierarchyCombineConcepts = FALSE,   automaticHierarchyCombineConceptsArgs = list(),   runAutomaticCorrelationCombineConcepts = FALSE,   automaticCorrelationCombineConceptsArgs = list(),   numCores = parallel::detectCores() - 1 )

title: Run CohortContrast Analysis — CohortContrast
text: cdm Connection database targetTable Table target cohort (tbl) controlTable Table control cohort (tbl) pathToResults Path results folder, can project's working directory domainsIncluded list CDM domains include prevalenceCutOff numeric > set, removes concepts present (target) prevalenceCutOff times topK numeric > set, keeps number features analysis. Maximum number features exported. presenceFilter numeric > set, removes features represented less given percentage complementaryMappingTable Mappingtable mapping concept_ids present, columns CONCEPT_ID, CONCEPT_NAME, NEW_CONCEPT_ID, NEW_CONCEPT_NAME, ABSTRACTION_LEVEL, TYPE runChi2YTests Boolean running CHI2Y test (chi-squared test two proportions Yates continuity correction). runLogitTests boolean logit-tests getAllAbstractions boolean creating abstractions' levels imported data, useful using GUI exploring data getSourceData boolean fetching source data maximumAbstractionLevel Maximum level abstraction allowed createOutputFiles Boolean creating output files, default value TRUE complName Name output study directory runRemoveTemporalBias Logical; `TRUE`, runs `removeTemporalBias()` optional post-processing step. removeTemporalBiasArgs list additional arguments passed `removeTemporalBias()` (example `ratio`, `alpha`, `domainsIncluded`, `removeIdentified`). Missing arguments default `ratio = 1`, `alpha = 0.05`, `domainsIncluded = NULL`, `removeIdentified = TRUE`. runAutomaticHierarchyCombineConcepts Logical; `TRUE`, runs `automaticHierarchyCombineConcepts()` temporal-bias processing. automaticHierarchyCombineConceptsArgs list additional arguments passed `automaticHierarchyCombineConcepts()` (example `abstraction_level`, `minDepthAllowed`, `allowOnlyMinors`). Missing arguments default `abstraction_level = -1`, `minDepthAllowed = 0`, `allowOnlyMinors = TRUE`. runAutomaticCorrelationCombineConcepts Logical; `TRUE`, runs `automaticCorrelationCombineConcepts()` hierarchy combining. automaticCorrelationCombineConceptsArgs list additional arguments passed `automaticCorrelationCombineConcepts()` (example `abstraction_level`, `minCorrelation`, `maxDaysInBetween`, `heritageDriftAllowed`). Missing arguments default `abstraction_level = -1`, `minCorrelation = 0.7`, `maxDaysInBetween = 1`, `heritageDriftAllowed = FALSE`. numCores Number cores allocate parallel processing

title: Run CohortContrast Analysis — CohortContrast
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ control <- data.frame(   cohort_definition_id = c(1, 1, 1, 1, 1),   subject_id = c(5325, 3743, 2980, 1512, 2168),   cohort_start_date = as.Date(c("1982-06-02", "1997-03-23",    "2004-09-29", "2006-08-11", "1977-06-25")),   cohort_end_date = as.Date(c("2019-03-17", "2018-10-07",    "2018-04-01", "2017-11-29", "2018-11-22")) )  target <- data.frame(   cohort_definition_id = c(1, 1, 1, 1, 1),   subject_id = c(4804, 4861, 1563, 2830, 1655),   cohort_start_date = as.Date(c("1997-03-23", "1982-06-02",    "1977-06-25", "2006-08-11", "2004-09-29")),   cohort_end_date = as.Date(c("2018-10-29", "2019-05-23",    "2019-04-20", "2019-01-14", "2019-05-24")) )  control$cohort_definition_id = 100 target$cohort_definition_id = 500  cohort = rbind(control, target)  con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), dbdir = CDMConnector::eunomia_dir("GiBleed")) DBI::dbExecute(con, "CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS example") DBI::dbWriteTable(con,   DBI::SQL('"example"."cohort"'), cohort)  cdm <- CDMConnector::cdmFromCon(con = con, cdmName = "eunomia",  cdmSchema = "main", writeSchema = "main")   targetTable <- cohortFromCohortTable(cdm = cdm, db = con,   tableName = "cohort", schemaName = 'example', cohortId = 500) controlTable <- cohortFromCohortTable(cdm = cdm, db = con,  tableName = "cohort", schemaName = 'example', cohortId = 100)   pathToResults = getwd()  data = CohortContrast(   cdm,   targetTable = targetTable,   controlTable = controlTable,   pathToResults,   domainsIncluded = c(     "Drug"   ),   prevalenceCutOff = 0.1,   topK = FALSE,   presenceFilter = 0.005,   complementaryMappingTable = NULL,   runChi2YTests = FALSE,   runLogitTests = FALSE,   createOutputFiles = FALSE,   numCores = 1 )  DBI::dbDisconnect(con) } # }

title: Function for automatically combining concepts by hierarchy mapping — automaticCorrelationCombineConcepts
text: data CohortContrastObject abstraction_level abstraction level use mapping minCorrelation minimum correlation use automatic concept combining maxDaysInBetween minimum days concepts use automatic concept combining heritageDriftAllowed boolean allowing heritage drift (combining concepts differing heritages)

what: Function for automatically combining concepts by hierarchy mapping — automaticHierarchyCombineConcepts
title: Function for automatically combining concepts by hierarchy mapping — automaticHierarchyCombineConcepts

TRUE, creates new virtual environment. Default TRUE. Set FALSE systems without python3-venv package. force Logical. TRUE, recreates virtual environment even exists. Default FALSE.

title: Insert cohort tables to CDM instance, preparing them from CohortContrast analysis — createCohortContrastCdm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ user <- Sys.getenv("DB_USERNAME") #TODO pw <- Sys.getenv("DB_PASSWORD") #TODO server <- stringr::str_c(Sys.getenv("DB_HOST"), "/", Sys.getenv("DB_NAME")) #TODO port <- Sys.getenv("DB_PORT") #TODO cdmSchema <-   Sys.getenv("OHDSI_CDM") cdmVocabSchema <-   Sys.getenv("OHDSI_VOCAB") cdmResultsSchema <-   Sys.getenv("OHDSI_RESULTS") writeSchema <-   Sys.getenv("OHDSI_WRITE") writePrefix <- "cc_"  db = DBI::dbConnect(   RPostgres::Postgres(),   dbname = Sys.getenv("DB_NAME"),   host = Sys.getenv("DB_HOST"),   user = Sys.getenv("DB_USERNAME"),   password = Sys.getenv("DB_PASSWORD"),   port  = port )  cdm <- CDMConnector::cdmFromCon(   con = db,   cdmSchema = cdmSchema,   achillesSchema = cdmResultsSchema,   writeSchema = c(schema = writeSchema, prefix = writePrefix), )   targetTable <- cohortFromCohortTable(cdm = cdm, db = db,  tableName = "cohort", schemaName = cdmResultsSchema, cohortId = 1) controlTable <- cohortFromCohortTable(cdm = cdm, db = db,  tableName = "cohort", schemaName = cdmResultsSchema, cohortId = 2)  cdm <- createCohortContrastCdm(cdm = cdm, targetTable = targetTable, controlTable = controlTable) } # }

what: Pre-compute Summary Data for a Study — precomputeSummary
title: Pre-compute Summary Data for a Study — precomputeSummary
text: Generates aggregated summary data patient-level parquet files, removing individual patient information. creates "summary mode" dataset can shared without privacy concerns.

title: Pre-compute Summary Data for a Study — precomputeSummary
code: precomputeSummary(   studyPath,   outputPath = NULL,   clusterKValues = c(2, 3, 4, 5),   conceptLimit = 60,   minCellCount = 0,   maxParallelJobs = 1,   minibatchKMeansCutoffPatients = 50000 )

title: Pre-compute Summary Data for a Study — precomputeSummary
text: studyPath Path directory containing patient-level parquet files (data_patients.parquet, data_features.parquet, etc.) outputPath Path output summary files. Default studyPath + "_summary" clusterKValues Vector k values pre-compute clustering . Default c(2, 3, 4, 5). conceptLimit Maximum number concepts use clustering. Default 60. minCellCount Minimum patient count small cell suppression (privacy). Counts 1 (minCellCount-1) rounded minCellCount. Default 0 (disabled). Set e.g. 5 apply suppression. maxParallelJobs Maximum number parallel clustering jobs. Default 1 (sequential) avoid --memory errors servers. Set 2-4 machines ample RAM faster execution. minibatchKMeansCutoffPatients target patient count greater value, clustering uses MiniBatchKMeans instead KMedoids. Default 50000.

title: Pre-compute Summary Data for a Study — precomputeSummary
text: list : outputPath Path generated summary directory. files Named list generated file paths metadata Study metadata including demographics clustering info

title: Pre-compute Summary Data for a Study — precomputeSummary
text: function pre-computes: - Concept-level time distribution statistics (violin/box plots) - Age gender statistics per concept - Ordinal concept summaries (1st, 2nd, 3rd occurrences) - Clustering results k=2,3,4,5 clusters - Cluster overlap differentiation metrics

title: Pre-compute Summary Data for a Study — precomputeSummary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Generate summary data for a study (no suppression) result <- precomputeSummary(   studyPath = "results_parquet/Breast_cancer",   outputPath = "summaries/Breast_cancer" )  # With small cell suppression (counts 1-4 become 5) result <- precomputeSummary(   studyPath = "results_parquet/Breast_cancer",   minCellCount = 5 )  # View generated files print(result$files)  # Run viewer with summary data (no patient data needed) runCohortContrastViewer(dataDir = "summaries") } # }

what: Remove Temporal Bias from CohortContrast Analysis — removeTemporalBias
title: Remove Temporal Bias from CohortContrast Analysis — removeTemporalBias
text: function identifies optionally removes concepts may represent temporal bias CohortContrast analysis. works creating age/sex matched controls general population time periods target cohort, using proportion test identify concepts matched cohort greater equal prevalence compared target. concepts likely represent temporal trends (e.g., seasonal effects, healthcare changes) rather condition-specific features.

title: Remove Temporal Bias from CohortContrast Analysis — removeTemporalBias
code: removeTemporalBias(   data,   cdm,   ratio = 1,   alpha = 0.05,   domainsIncluded = NULL,   removeIdentified = FALSE )

title: Remove Temporal Bias from CohortContrast Analysis — removeTemporalBias
text: data CohortContrast result object (returned CohortContrast function) cdm Connection database (package CDMConnector) ratio Matching ratio control cohort generation (default: 1) alpha Significance level proportion test Bonferroni correction (default: 0.05) domainsIncluded Domains analyze temporal bias (default: original analysis) removeIdentified TRUE, automatically remove identified temporal bias concepts data (default: FALSE)

title: Remove Temporal Bias from CohortContrast Analysis — removeTemporalBias
text: list containing: temporal_bias_concepts data frame concepts identified potential temporal bias data original filtered CohortContrast data object (removeIdentified = TRUE) matched_control_prevalences Prevalence data matched control cohort

title: Remove Temporal Bias from CohortContrast Analysis — removeTemporalBias
text: function applies Bonferroni correction multiple testing, adjusting significance level dividing alpha number concepts tested.

title: Remove Temporal Bias from CohortContrast Analysis — removeTemporalBias
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Run CohortContrast analysis data <- CohortContrast(cdm, targetTable, controlTable, pathToResults, ...)  # Identify temporal bias concepts result <- removeTemporalBias(data, cdm, ratio = 1)  # View identified concepts print(result$temporal_bias_concepts)  # Remove identified concepts and get filtered data result_filtered <- removeTemporalBias(data, cdm, removeIdentified = TRUE) filtered_data <- result_filtered$data } # }

title: Run CohortContrast Viewer Dashboard — runCohortContrastViewer
text: Launches CohortContrast Viewer interactive dashboard.

title: Run CohortContrast Viewer Dashboard — runCohortContrastViewer
code: runCohortContrastViewer(   dataDir = NULL,   port = 8050,   host = "127.0.0.1",   mode = c("simple", "server", "debug"),   logFile = NULL,   allowExports = TRUE,   openBrowser = TRUE,   background = TRUE )

title: Run CohortContrast Viewer Dashboard — runCohortContrastViewer
text: dataDir Path directory containing parquet data files. NULL, defaults current working directory (`getwd()`). port Port number Dash server. Default 8050. host Host address. Default "127.0.0.1" (localhost). mode Run mode. One `"simple"`, `"server"`, `"debug"`. `"simple"` hides debug-style output default. `"server"` enables file logging suitable hosted/server runs. `"debug"` enables maximum debug features. logFile Optional log file path `"server"` (`"debug"`) mode. NULL `"server"` mode, defaults `file.path(dataDir, "contrast_viewer.log")`. allowExports Logical. FALSE, disables export actions (TSV/PNG) UI. Default TRUE. openBrowser Logical. TRUE, opens dashboard default browser. Default TRUE. background Logical. TRUE, runs server background. Default TRUE.

title: Run CohortContrast Viewer Dashboard — runCohortContrastViewer
text: background TRUE, invisibly returns process object.   FALSE, blocks server stopped.
